section_title: When an agent has, without authority, done acts or incurred obligations
content:
237. When an agent has, without authority, done acts or incurred obligations
principal
to third persons on behalf of his principal, the principal is bound by such acts
inducing
belief that or obligations if he has by his words or conduct induced such third persons to
agent's
believe that such acts and obligations were within the scope of the agent's
unauthorized
acts were authority.
authorized
Illustrations
(a) A consigns goods to B for sale, and gives him instructions not to sell
under a fixed price. C, being ignorant of B's instructions, enters into a
contract with B to buy the goods at a price lower than the reserved price. A is
bound by the contract.
(b) A entrusts B with negotiable instruments endorsed in blank. B sells them
to C in violation of private orders from A. The sale is good.
Effect, on
